name: SpiderFoot
description: Automated OSINT engine that collects intelligence and maps attack surface from 200+ modules.
bestFor: Automated multi-source OSINT pivoting from a single seed (email, name, domain, IP, phone, username) with correlation
trustNote: 18k+ stars, long-standing, widely used OSINT automation engine; open-source core (SpiderFoot HX is the paid SaaS).
notes: Run locally via web UI (python sf.py -l 127.0.0.1:5001) or CLI. Many modules require their own API keys. Set scan to passive-only modules to avoid touching the target. Excellent first-pass aggregator for a known selector.

name: OSINT Framework
description: Web-based directory tree of OSINT tools and resources organized by data type.
bestFor: Browsing categorized OSINT resources by selector type (username, email, phone, images, etc.) to pick the right tool
trustNote: 11.5k+ stars; the canonical OSINT resource tree, also hosted at osintframework.com.
notes: Not a tool itself but a curated navigable index. Useful as a frontier/discovery surface for new tools by category. Many leaf links are external sites of varying quality.

name: gowitness
description: Go web screenshot utility using Chrome Headless for bulk page capture.
bestFor: Bulk screenshotting lists of URLs/hosts to visually triage and preserve evidence
trustNote: SensePost (Orange Cyberdefense) project, 4.3k+ stars.
notes: Actively connects to targets to render pages. Useful for capturing a subject's web presence (personal site, profile pages) as evidence, but it's infra-oriented.

name: nox-framework
description: OSINT/CTI framework for automated identity pivoting and risk analysis across 120+ sources.
bestFor: Automated identity pivoting across many sources from a seed selector
trustNote: ~190 stars; newer framework, verify module behavior before relying on it.
notes: Claims 120+ sources for identity pivoting. Less battle-tested than SpiderFoot; validate which sources it actually queries and their opsec profile.

name: awesome-recon-tools
description: Compiled list of tools for reconnaissance and footprinting.
bestFor: Discovering recon/footprinting tools grouped by domain/network vs personal/email
trustNote: Curated awesome-list, ~170 stars.
notes: Meta-resource; the personal-information/email-footprinting section lists people-relevant tools (PeekYou, BeenVerified, Sherlock, theHarvester, Infoga).

name: Argos OSINT Workstation
description: Script that automatically sets up an OSINT workstation on Ubuntu.
bestFor: Provisioning a ready-to-use OSINT investigation VM/workstation
trustNote: ~164 stars; community OSINT ops project.
notes: Installs a bundle of OSINT tools onto Ubuntu. Useful for standing up an investigator environment; review the install script before running.

name: PhoNumSpy
description: Phone number OSINT tool returning location, country code, city, carrier plus web and social-network footprint search.
bestFor: Enriching a phone number with carrier/location data and finding linked web/social footprint
trustNote: ~46 stars; small community tool, verify results.
notes: Also searches temporary/disposable phone number providers, which can flag burner numbers. Performs web and social searches, so it touches third-party sites (active).

name: aarya
description: Email-to-digital-footprint tool that validates an email across platforms and extracts metadata (e.g. Google Maps contributions/reviews).
bestFor: Mapping which online platforms an email is registered on and pulling Google Maps reviews/contributions tied to it
trustNote: ~33 stars; community tool, validate platform checks.
notes: Checks Instagram, Wattpad, Duolingo and others for the email; Google Maps contributions can reveal places a subject has visited/reviewed. Touches target platforms (active).

name: helix
description: Advanced asynchronous OSINT identity mapper and relation tool.
bestFor: Mapping relationships between identities/selectors asynchronously
trustNote: ~19 stars; newer/small project.
notes: Identity/relationship mapper; potentially useful for building an associate graph around a subject. Small project, verify which sources it queries.

name: maldevel/osint
description: Tools, scripts and tips useful during OSINT investigations and reconnaissance.
bestFor: Reference collection of OSINT scripts and investigative tips
trustNote: ~13 stars; personal curated collection.
notes: Reference repo of scripts and tips rather than a single tool.

name: YoungerSibling
description: OSINT CLI tool with web scraping, DNS lookups, EXIF extraction, and username availability checks.
bestFor: Quick combined recon: username availability, DNS, and EXIF from images
trustNote: ~3 stars; educational personal project.
notes: Bundles username checks and EXIF extraction (image geolocation). Educational; small project.

name: DELETEME (digital footprint analyzer)
description: Find and analyze your digital footprint; Sherlock-inspired, privacy-focused.
bestFor: Enumerating accounts tied to a username across sites (privacy/footprint angle)
trustNote: ~1 star; small personal project inspired by Sherlock.
notes: Sherlock-style username enumeration. Prefer the original Sherlock for maturity.

name: PhantomTrace
description: Python OSINT tool aggregating public data from 35+ platforms.
bestFor: Aggregating public profile/account data from many platforms in one run
trustNote: 0 stars; unverified small project.
notes: Claims 35+ platforms. Unverified; validate before use.

name: Reconnor
description: Educational recon and OSINT tool suite.
bestFor: Learning/basic recon across DNS, web, and OSINT tasks
trustNote: ~2 stars; educational project.
notes: Educational tool suite; low maturity.

name: Osint-Harvester
description: Lightweight OSINT toolkit that collects and normalizes intelligence from multiple sources.
bestFor: Collecting and normalizing OSINT from several sources into a unified output
trustNote: ~1 star; small project.
notes: Lightweight collector/normalizer; unverified.

name: Ary-Recon
description: Web-based directory of passive reconnaissance tools.
bestFor: Browsing passive recon tools by category
trustNote: 0 stars; small directory page.
notes: Meta-resource directory of passive recon tools.

name: Aquatone
description: Tool for visual inspection of websites across a large number of hosts.
bestFor: Bulk visual recon/screenshotting of many hosts for triage
trustNote: Well-known recon tool by Michael Henriksen.
notes: Infra-focused. Listed in awesome-recon-tools.

name: BuiltWith
description: Scans websites and reports the web technologies they use (46k+ technologies).
bestFor: Profiling the tech stack of a website (could link a subject's sites by shared trackers/IDs)
trustNote: Established commercial service with a free lookup tier.
notes: Relationship lookups (shared Google Analytics/AdSense IDs across sites) can link a person's web properties; that feature is mostly paid.

name: Censys
description: Searchable global internet dataset for enumerating hosts, certs, and assets.
bestFor: Finding hosts, certificates and services tied to a domain/IP
trustNote: Major internet-scanning data provider; free tier with limits.
notes: Passive against the target (queries Censys's own scans). Infra-focused.

name: DNSDumpster
description: Free domain research tool to discover hosts and DNS records related to a domain.
bestFor: Mapping subdomains and DNS records of a domain passively
trustNote: Widely used Hacker Target service.
notes: Passive subdomain/DNS discovery. Infra-focused.

name: FindSubDomains (SecurityTrails)
description: Tool to discover subdomains of a domain.
bestFor: Subdomain enumeration
trustNote: Part of SecurityTrails.
notes: Infra-focused subdomain discovery.

name: Netcraft
description: Site reports, DNS search, and hosting history tools.
bestFor: Site report / hosting history / DNS lookups for a domain
trustNote: Long-established internet research company.
notes: Infra-focused; hosting history can occasionally aid attribution.

name: Shodan
description: Search engine for internet-connected devices and services.
bestFor: Finding exposed devices/services by IP, domain, or banner
trustNote: Industry-standard device search engine; free tier limited.
notes: Passive against target. Webcams/devices occasionally surface geolocation, but largely infra.

name: Wappalyzer
description: Identifies technologies used on websites.
bestFor: Fingerprinting a website's tech stack
trustNote: Popular tech-profiling extension/service.
notes: Browser extension; infra-focused.

name: Wireshark
description: The most widely used network protocol analyzer.
bestFor: Capturing and analyzing network traffic
trustNote: Industry-standard open-source analyzer.
notes: Network forensics tool; low relevance to people-finding.

name: Maltego
description: OSINT and graphical link-analysis tool for gathering and connecting information.
bestFor: Visual link analysis connecting people, emails, domains, social profiles via transforms
trustNote: Industry-standard link-analysis platform; free Community Edition with limits, paid for full transforms.
notes: Community Edition is free but limited (12 entities/transform, registration required). Excellent for building an entity/relationship graph around a missing person from many data sources.

name: recon-ng
description: Full-featured web reconnaissance framework with a modular Metasploit-like interface.
bestFor: Modular automated web recon and OSINT data gathering across many modules
trustNote: Long-standing, well-known recon framework by Tim Tomes.
notes: Module-driven; many modules need API keys. Strong for email/contact harvesting; some modules are active against targets.

name: theHarvester
description: Gathers emails, subdomains, hosts, names, and URLs from public sources/search engines.
bestFor: Harvesting emails and names tied to a domain from many public sources
trustNote: Standard recon tool bundled in Kali; the Kali wiki link in the source is dated, canonical repo is laramies/theHarvester.
notes: Pulls emails/names from search engines and other sources for a domain; can surface employee/contact names tied to an org a subject is associated with.

name: Sherlock
description: Hunts down social media accounts by username across hundreds of sites.
bestFor: Enumerating a username across 400+ social/web platforms
trustNote: Flagship username-enumeration tool, very widely used.
notes: Connects to each platform to check account existence (active). One of the highest-value tools here for finding a subject's accounts from a known handle.

name: Infoga
description: Gathers email OSINT: domains, sources, and breach exposure for email addresses.
bestFor: Finding where an email appears publicly and whether it is in known breaches
trustNote: Known but somewhat dated tool by m4ll0k.
notes: May require maintenance/API updates to run; uses search engines and Haveibeenpwned-style sources.

name: PeekYou
description: People-search engine that locates personal info from family members to social media accounts.
bestFor: Finding a person's profiles, relatives, and basic info by name/username
trustNote: Established people-search aggregator; data quality varies and is US-skewed.
notes: Free search results; deeper reports may upsell to paid partners. Good for an initial name/username people-search pass, mainly US coverage.

name: BeenVerified
description: Background-check service aggregating public records (addresses, relatives, phone, etc.).
bestFor: US background checks combining addresses, relatives, phones and records
trustNote: Commercial US data broker; full reports are paid (subscription).
notes: Search is free but full reports require a paid subscription; included as freemium for awareness. US-only. Subject to FCRA restrictions.

name: Followerwonk
description: Analytics tool that scrapes and analyzes Twitter/X profile and follower data.
bestFor: Analyzing a Twitter/X account's bio data and follower relationships
trustNote: Established Twitter analytics tool (Moz); value reduced by X API restrictions.
notes: Usefulness depends on current X/Twitter API access; free tier limited.

name: Fierce
description: DNS reconnaissance tool for locating non-contiguous IP space and hostnames.
bestFor: DNS recon / subdomain and IP-space discovery for a domain
trustNote: Classic DNS recon tool, bundled in Kali.
notes: Infra-focused DNS reconnaissance.

name: ronin-recon
description: Recursive recon engine and framework for subdomains, DNS, port scanning, and TLS certs.
bestFor: Recursive automated infrastructure recon
trustNote: Part of the Ronin Ruby security toolkit.
notes: Infra-focused recursive recon framework.

name: Ghost Eye
description: Information-gathering tool for Whois, DNS, EtherApe, Nmap, and more.
bestFor: Quick combined Whois/DNS/Nmap recon on a target
trustNote: Community recon wrapper.
notes: Wrapper around standard recon utilities; infra-focused.

name: r3con1z3r
description: Lightweight web information-gathering tool written in Python.
bestFor: Quick passive web/domain info gathering with an HTML report
trustNote: Small community recon tool.
notes: Lightweight footprinting reporter; infra-focused.

name: orb
description: Massive footprinting tool.
bestFor: Broad automated footprinting of a target
trustNote: By epsylon (Psy); niche.
notes: Broad footprinting utility; infra-focused.

name: OTE (OSINT Template Engine)
description: OSINT template engine / cross-platform subdomain enumeration and recon GUI.
bestFor: GUI-driven subdomain enumeration and OSINT recon
trustNote: ~575 stars; C++/Qt recon GUI.
notes: Desktop GUI for subdomain/host enumeration; infra-focused.
